title: Arithmetic
description: Learn and master the concepts of Arithmetic with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Pre-Algebra
description: This is where students transition from arithmetic to algebraic thinking.

title: Algebra I
description: Learn and master the concepts of Algebra I with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Algebra II
description: Learn and master the concepts of Algebra II with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Geometry
description: Learn and master the concepts of Geometry with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Trigonometry
description: Learn and master the concepts of Trigonometry with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Precalculus
description: This is a university-level **Trigonometry** course. It takes students from basic right-triangle trigonometry all the way to polar coordinates, complex numbers in polar form, and introductory Fourier concepts, making it a solid foundation for **Precalculus** and **Calculus**.

title: Calculus I
description: This is a **complete Precalculus** course. Its only purpose is to make students fully prepared for **Calculus I**, where limits, derivatives, and integrals are developed rigorously.

title: Calculus II
description: Everything in **Calculus II** builds directly on these topics.

title: Calculus III (Multivariable Calculus)
description: This is the standard **Calculus II** curriculum. It prepares students for **Calculus III**, where the focus shifts to **multivariable calculus**—functions of several variables, partial derivatives, multiple integrals, vector calculus, and line/surface integrals.

title: Linear Algebra
description: Learn and master the concepts of Linear Algebra with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Differential Equations
description: Learn and master the concepts of Differential Equations with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Discrete Mathematics
description: These are advanced standalone subjects rather than direct continuations of the calculus sequence.

title: Probability
description: This is the standard **Discrete Mathematics** curriculum used in Computer Science degrees. It serves as the mathematical backbone for algorithms, data structures, automata theory, cryptography, databases, and many advanced CS subjects.

title: Statistics
description: Learn and master the concepts of Statistics with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Number Theory
description: These are advanced pure mathematics subjects rather than applied statistics.

title: Graph Theory
description: Learn and master the concepts of Graph Theory with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Real Analysis
description: Learn and master the concepts of Real Analysis with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Abstract Algebra
description: This is the standard undergraduate **Real Analysis** curriculum. It provides the rigorous mathematical foundation for advanced calculus, topology, measure theory, and functional analysis. It's one of the core pure mathematics courses after the introductory calculus sequence.

title: Complex Analysis
description: This is the standard undergraduate **Abstract Algebra** course. It is much more proof-oriented than earlier mathematics courses and provides the algebraic foundation for advanced mathematics, cryptography, and theoretical computer science. The final major course in your core math sequence would be **Complex Analysis**.
